channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Iran rejects Trump's proposal to end the war and lays out its own terms
item.description: Iran's military command is rejecting a 15-point peace proposal from the U.S., saying it will not "come to terms" with Washington and laid out its own conditions.
item.content:encoded: Iran's military command is rejecting a 15-point peace proposal from the U.S., saying it will not "come to terms" with Washington and laid out its own conditions.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Estrin

item.title: Former deputy special envoy for Iran on negotiations between US and Iran to end war
item.description: As the U.S. and Iran trade demands for ending the war, NPR's Steve Inskeep talks to Richard Nephew, a former deputy special envoy for Iran in the Biden administration.
item.content:encoded: As the U.S. and Iran trade demands for ending the war, NPR's Steve Inskeep talks to Richard Nephew, a former deputy special envoy for Iran in the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Travelers face longest TSA wait times in history as agency's head warns of closures
item.description: The acting TSA administrator told Congress Wednesday that the agency faces a "dire" situation and may have to close smaller airports, as many security officers working without pay quit or call out.
item.content:encoded: The acting TSA administrator told Congress Wednesday that the agency faces a "dire" situation and may have to close smaller airports, as many security officers working without pay quit or call out.
item.dc:creator: Hosts

item.title: It's Equal Pay Day. Women have lost ground for the second year in a row
item.description: The annual observance marks how far into the new year women must work to make what men earned in the previous year. This year, it's March 26, a day later than it was in 2025.
item.content:encoded:
The annual observance marks how far into the new year women must work to make what men earned in the previous year. This year, it's March 26, a day later than it was in 2025.

(Image credit: simplehappyart)
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu

item.title: Hoping to unseat Collins, Maine Democrats battle it out in an expensive U.S. Senate primary
item.description: As June's primary election nears, Democratic Gov. Janet Mills and combat veteran Graham Platner are effectively engaged in a proxy battle between factions in their own party.
item.content:encoded:
As June's primary election nears, Democratic Gov. Janet Mills and combat veteran Graham Platner are effectively engaged in a proxy battle between factions in their own party.

(Image credit: Robert F. Bukaty/AP; Kevin Dietsch/Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Steve Mistler

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Iran rejects U.S. peace proposal and lays out its own conditions, the Army's 82nd Airborne Division readies to deploy to Iran, jury finds Meta and Google liable in social media addiction trial.
item.content:encoded: Iran rejects U.S. peace proposal and lays out its own conditions, the Army's 82nd Airborne Division readies to deploy to Iran, jury finds Meta and Google liable in social media addiction trial.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: For his first solo album, Flea trades in his bass for his first love -- jazz trumpet
item.description: Known for playing bass guitar in the Red Hot Chili Peppers, Flea is releasing his first solo album -- and it features his first love: jazz trumpet. It's called "Honora."
item.content:encoded: Known for playing bass guitar in the Red Hot Chili Peppers, Flea is releasing his first solo album -- and it features his first love: jazz trumpet. It's called "Honora."
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: The human and financial costs rack up as immigration detention expands
item.dc:creator: Martin Kaste

item.title: Dem Emily Gregory on flipping Florida House seat that includes Trump's Mar-a-Lago
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with state Representative-elect Emily Gregory who won a special legislative election in Florida's 87th District, home to President Trump's Mar-a-Lago resort.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with state Representative-elect Emily Gregory who won a special legislative election in Florida's 87th District, home to President Trump's Mar-a-Lago resort.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Trades made before Trump delayed plans to attack Iran raise insider trading concerns
item.description: Minutes before President Trump delayed plans to attack Iran's energy infrastructure, big trades were made, raising insider trading concerns. NPR's A Martinez asks economist Paul Krugman.
item.content:encoded: Minutes before President Trump delayed plans to attack Iran's energy infrastructure, big trades were made, raising insider trading concerns. NPR's A Martinez asks economist Paul Krugman.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez
